name: Jewish Autonomous Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the field, evoking peace and the snowy expanses of the Russian Far East

description: A white field crossed by seven thin horizontal stripes through the middle, in rainbow order from top to bottom: red, orange, yellow, green, light-blue, blue and violet.
history: Adopted on 23 July 1996 by the Legislative Assembly of the Jewish Autonomous Oblast; the rainbow design references both the biblical Noahic covenant and the seven-branched menorah, symbols associated with the Jewish character of the oblast.

features.symbolism: A white field bearing a horizontal rainbow of seven thin parallel stripes in the centre

a horizontal rainbow of seven narrow stripes (red, orange, yellow, green, light-blue, blue, violet) recalling the seven days of creation, the seven branches of the menorah and the rainbow as the biblical sign of God's covenant
